requirements.title: Project boundary
requirements.statement: Every user-facing research object belongs to exactly one Project unless explicitly promoted to a shared library.

requirements.title: Three primary surfaces
requirements.statement: A Project exposes Chat, Documents, and Sources as its primary surfaces.

requirements.title: Persistent chat
requirements.statement: Messages, branches, attachments, tool events, and streaming output survive reload and reconnect.

requirements.title: Visible source scope
requirements.statement: Chat exposes and honors the active authorized source scope.

requirements.title: Canonical Markdown
requirements.statement: Documents use stable blocks with deterministic Markdown serialization as the durable interchange authority.

requirements.title: Typed patches
requirements.statement: AI changes are typed, reviewable, and carry an expected base revision.

requirements.title: Locked content
requirements.statement: Agents cannot silently overwrite locked or published content.

requirements.title: Immutable source versions
requirements.statement: Each ingestion or synchronization creates an immutable SourceVersion.

requirements.title: Explicit capabilities
requirements.statement: Every source reports stored, inspectable, searchable, citable, and richly-understood capability independently.

requirements.title: No silent deletion
requirements.statement: Agents cannot silently delete user-provided sources.

requirements.title: Exact evidence locator
requirements.statement: Every citation resolves to an immutable source version and exact locator.

requirements.title: Claim status
requirements.statement: Every factual claim is supported, corroborated, disputed, inferred, unsupported, stale, removed, or unresolved.

requirements.title: Derived material
requirements.statement: Generated summaries cannot independently corroborate their upstream claims.

requirements.title: Hybrid retrieval
requirements.statement: Retrieval combines authorization, lexical, semantic, metadata, entity, scholarly, and code-aware methods as applicable.

requirements.title: Authorization before retrieval
requirements.statement: Unauthorized candidate text never reaches a model or reranker.

requirements.title: Capability model registry
requirements.statement: Model selection resolves through product roles and current capabilities rather than hardcoded UI model IDs.

requirements.title: Provider adapters
requirements.statement: Provider-specific model and research capabilities remain behind replaceable adapters.

requirements.title: Durable research run
requirements.statement: Deep research is persisted, resumable, interruptible, budgeted, and auditable.

requirements.title: Research contract
requirements.statement: Long research begins with an explicit contract covering scope, sources, output, budgets, and completion criteria.

requirements.title: Long-form section contracts
requirements.statement: Large outputs are assembled from bounded section contracts and global consistency audits.

requirements.title: Bounded workers
requirements.statement: Every agent worker receives immutable scope, tools, budgets, schema, and prohibited actions.

requirements.title: Inspectable memory
requirements.statement: Project memory is versioned, attributable, editable, and never substitutes for required evidence.

requirements.title: No-drift propagation
requirements.statement: Source changes revalidate dependent claims and produce the smallest safe document patches.

requirements.title: Single canonical projection
requirements.statement: Public and private documents are projections of one canonical revision and evidence graph.

requirements.title: Immutable publication
requirements.statement: A publication creates an immutable snapshot with rights, privacy, citation, and support validation.

requirements.title: Commit-pinned repository evidence
requirements.statement: Repository citations include repository, commit SHA, path, and line range.

requirements.title: Reviewable repository edits
requirements.statement: Repository changes are sandboxed, tested, approved, committed to a branch, and opened as a draft pull request.

requirements.title: Asynchronous operations
requirements.statement: Long-running API commands return addressable Operations with polling, SSE, cancellation, approvals, and stable errors.

requirements.title: Safe mutations
requirements.statement: Mutations support idempotency and expected-base-version concurrency.

requirements.title: Untrusted source content
requirements.statement: Source content cannot redefine system instructions, permissions, tools, or approval policy.

requirements.title: Least privilege
requirements.statement: Connectors and agents receive the minimum short-lived capabilities required for one operation.

requirements.title: Content minimization
requirements.statement: Analytics, logs, support systems, and caches do not become shadow stores of private Project content.

requirements.title: Auditability
requirements.statement: Every privileged action, model call, tool action, source decision, revision, and publication is attributable.

requirements.title: Recovery
requirements.statement: The system defines and tests restore, reconciliation, degradation, and publication-withdrawal procedures.

requirements.title: Accessible interface
requirements.statement: Primary workflows and generated outputs meet WCAG 2.2 AA expectations.

requirements.title: International research
requirements.statement: Schemas, retrieval, editing, citations, and exports support Unicode, locale-neutral APIs, multilingual content, and RTL presentation.

requirements.title: Product truth
requirements.statement: Launch claims must match verified runtime, policy, support, billing, accessibility, security, and recovery evidence.
